docs.title: RighToGo
docs.text:
RighToGo is a Kubex Ecosystem VSCode extension for running Go scripts quickly, safely, and with minimal setup friction.

Goal: enable short Go experimentation cycles, with or without a full project structure.

docs.title: What it solves
docs.text:
• Avoids manual go.mod setup for standalone scripts.
  • Keeps interactive execution (stdin/stdout) through the integrated terminal.
  • Enforces eligibility checks to prevent incorrect execution of library files.
  • Provides a path for LLM/MCP-assisted diagnostics (MVP stub).

docs.title: High-level flow
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  A["Go file opened"] --> B{"go.mod in directory?"}
  B -->|"Yes"| C["go run file.go"]
  B -->|"No"| D["Create temp directory"]
  D --> E["go mod init"]
  E --> F["go mod tidy"]
  F --> G["go run file.go"]
  G --> H["Temporary cleanup"]

docs.title: Main commands
docs.text:
• RighToGo: Run Current Go Script
  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (With Args)
  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (New Window)
  • RighToGo: Ask LLM About This Script

docs.title: RighToGo
docs.text: RighToGo é uma extensão para VSCode criada para reduzir a fricção de executar scripts Go efêmeros. O objetivo é permitir ciclos rápidos de teste sem obrigar setup manual de projeto em cada snippet.

docs.title: Propósito
docs.text:
• Executar scripts Go com um clique em contexto de editor.
  • Eliminar o bloqueio de go.mod para arquivos soltos.
  • Preservar experiência interativa com stdin e stdout via Terminal Integrado.

docs.title: 1) Arquivo dentro de projeto com go.mod
docs.text:
• A extensão detecta go.mod no diretório do arquivo.
  • Executa go run <arquivo.go> no RighToGo Terminal.

docs.title: 2) Arquivo solto sem go.mod
docs.text:
• Cria diretório temporário.
  • Copia o arquivo Go.
  • Executa sequência:
  • go mod init rightogo_temp_run
  • go mod tidy
  • go run <arquivo.go>
  • Remove o diretório temporário ao final quando rightogo.cleanupTemporaryDirectory = true (default).

docs.title: Comandos
docs.text:
• RighToGo: Run Current Go Script
  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (With Args)
  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (New Window)
  • RighToGo: Ask LLM About This Script (stub local, sem rede)

docs.title: Configurações
docs.text:
• rightogo.goBinaryPath: caminho do binário Go.
  • default: ${userHome}/.go/bin/go
  • suporte a ${userHome}, ${env:VAR}, ${workspaceFolder}, ~
  • rightogo.cleanupTemporaryDirectory: remove pasta temporária após execução.
  • default: true
  • rightogo.promptForArgumentsOnRun: abre input de argumentos no comando padrão.
  • default: false
  • rightogo.runInNewWindowTerminalByDefault: tenta mover terminal para nova janela.
  • default: false

docs.title: Build, Teste e Execução Local
docs.text:
1. pnpm install
  2. pnpm run compile
  3. pnpm test
  4. Pressione F5 no VSCode para abrir o Extension Development Host.
  5. Abra um .go com package main e use o botão Play no título do editor.

docs.title: Real Cases de Produtividade
docs.text:
• Teste rápido de parser, regex, payload transformer ou helper de infra sem criar projeto completo.
  • Reproduzir bug em script curto com dependência externa e validar correção em minutos.
  • Rodar snippets de troubleshooting com entrada interativa (fmt.Scanln) direto no terminal.
  • Criar prova de conceito local para API/client e descartar ambiente efêmero automaticamente.

docs.title: Integração nativa e interativa com GoSetup (Kubex-Ecosystem)
docs.text:
Quando o Go não estiver disponível no host, RighToGo deixará de apenas emitir erro e oferecerá provisionamento seguro no perfil do usuário.

Fluxo proposto:

  1. Buscar versões em https://go.dev/dl/?mode=json.
  2. Extrair version e remover prefixo go (ex.: go1.26.0 -> 1.26.0).
  3. Mostrar versões em vscode.window.showQuickPick.
  4. Ao confirmar, abrir Terminal Integrado e executar:
  5. Unix/Mac:
    • bash -c "$(curl -sSfL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubex-ecosystem/gosetup/main/go.sh')" -s install <VERSAO_ESCOLHIDA>
  6. Windows:
    • fluxo equivalente em PowerShell usando:
    • https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubex-ecosystem/gosetup/main/go.ps1
    • comando install <VERSAO_ESCOLHIDA>

Resultado esperado da V2:

  • Resiliência total no onboarding do runtime Go.
  • Operação batteries-included sem depender de setup manual prévio.
  • Preservação de controle explícito do usuário sobre versão instalada.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Run Current Go Script (With Args) for fast argument input.
  • Run Current Go Script (New Window) to attempt terminal focus in a separate window.
  • Editor and Explorer context-menu entries for direct execution.
  • rightogo.promptForArgumentsOnRun setting.
  • rightogo.runInNewWindowTerminalByDefault setting.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • rightogo.goBinaryPath now supports placeholders (${userHome}, ${env:VAR}, ${workspaceFolder}, ~) with resilient fallback resolution.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Active Go script execution command.
  • Project-mode execution (go.mod present).
  • Ephemeral mode for standalone files (go mod init, go mod tidy, go run).
  • Configurable temporary cleanup.
  • Ask LLM command with local stub.
  • Initial automated tests for eligibility and ephemeral sequence.
  • MkDocs Material doc-site base.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Standardized build output in dist/.
  • .vsix packaging output in bin/.
  • Build scripts include optional TypeScript-Go (tsgo) path.

docs.title: Guidelines
docs.text:
• Keep PRs focused and small.
  • Preserve compatibility with terminal-based execution flow.
  • Do not introduce breaking changes without prior proposal.

docs.title: Recommended flow
docs.text:
git checkout -b feat/my-change
pnpm install
pnpm run check-types
pnpm run lint
pnpm test

docs.title: PR checklist
docs.text:
• Tests passing
  • Documentation updated
  • Build references aligned with dist/
  • If applicable, validate packaging in bin/*.vsix

docs.title: Current collaboration scope
docs.text:
• V1: robustness and maintainability.
  • V2: interactive GoSetup integration when Go is unavailable.

See the official repository license file:

  • https://github.com/kubex-ecosystem/rightogo/blob/main/LICENSE

docs.title: Current extension points
docs.text:
• VSCode commands registered in activate().
  • Internal service modules for eligibility and ephemeral sequence planning.
  • Local bridge for LLM/MCP analysis (MVP stub).

docs.title: Relevant internal contracts
docs.text:
isMainPackageScript(fileText: string): boolean
buildEphemeralCommandSequence(goBinaryPath: string, targetFileName: string): CommandInvocation[]
executeEphemeralCommandSequence(sequence, executor): Promise<void>
parseProgramArguments(rawInput: string): ParsedArgsResult

docs.title: Evolution strategy
docs.text:
• Keep pure contracts for testability.
  • Isolate shell generation and process execution logic.
  • Evolve llmBridge into provider-agnostic integration.

docs.title: Core modules
docs.text:
• extension.ts: command orchestration and runtime flow.
  • services/scriptEligibility.ts: executable file checks (package main).
  • services/ephemeralRunner.ts: ephemeral command sequence builder.
  • services/programArgs.ts: quick-input argument parsing.
  • test/suite/*: behavioral regression coverage.

docs.title: Structural decisions
docs.text:
1. Integrated Terminal as the primary execution channel.
  2. Temporary cleanup enabled by default.
  3. Dual validation (UI context + runtime command gate).
  4. Local LLM/MCP stub in MVP.

docs.title: Logical flow
docs.text:
flowchart TB
  A["VSCode Command"] --> B["Validation Gate"]
  B --> C{"go.mod present?"}
  C -->|"yes"| D["Project Runner"]
  C -->|"no"| E["Ephemeral Runner"]
  D --> F["Integrated Terminal"]
  E --> F
  F --> G["Execution State"]
  G --> H["LLM Stub Context"]

docs.title: Strategic V2: Interactive GoSetup
docs.text:
When Go is not found:

  1. Fetch versions from https://go.dev/dl/?mode=json.
  2. Parse version and remove go prefix.
  3. Display options in showQuickPick.
  4. Execute installation using Kubex Ecosystem GoSetup scripts.

Planned commands:

Unix/MacWindows (PowerShell) 
bash -c "$(curl -sSfL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubex-ecosystem/gosetup/main/go.sh')" -s install <VERSION>

# Equivalent flow using go.ps1
# https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubex-ecosystem/gosetup/main/go.ps1

docs.title: Local setup
docs.text:
pnpm install
pnpm run compile
pnpm test

docs.title: Run extension in development mode
docs.text:
• Open the workspace in VSCode.
  • Press F5.
  • Validate RighToGo: Run Current Go Script and related commands.

docs.title: Linting and type checks
docs.text:
pnpm run check-types
pnpm run lint

docs.title: Packaging
docs.text:
pnpm run package
pnpm run vscode:build

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
• TypeScript build output: dist/
  • Distribution package: bin/*.vsix

docs.title: Documentation commands
docs.text:
make build-docs
make serve-docs

docs.title: TypeScript Scripts
docs.text: This repository supports a standard tsc pipeline and an optional TypeScript-Go (tsgo) pipeline when available on the developer machine.

docs.title: Available scripts
docs.text:
Standard pipeline (tsc)Alternative pipeline (tsgo) 
pnpm run check-types
pnpm run compile
pnpm run watch

pnpm run check-types:tsgo
pnpm run compile:tsgo
pnpm run watch:tsgo

docs.title: Full build
docs.text:
With tscWith tsgo 
pnpm run build

pnpm run build:tsgo

docs.title: Artifacts
docs.text:
• Compiled extension output: dist/
  • VSCode package (.vsix): bin/

docs.title: Overall status
docs.text: The MVP is implemented, compiled, and tested. Primary execution uses the Integrated Terminal to support full stdin/stdout behavior.

docs.title: Current module map (src/)
docs.text:
• src/extension.ts
  • Extension entrypoint.
  • Registers commands:
    • rightogo.runScript
    • rightogo.runScriptWithArgs
    • rightogo.runScriptInNewWindow
    • rightogo.askLlmAboutScript

  • Coordinates validation, mode selection (project vs ephemeral), terminal execution, and run monitoring.

  • src/services/scriptEligibility.ts

  • extractFirstCodeLine(fileText)

  • isMainPackageScript(fileText)

  • src/services/ephemeralRunner.ts

  • buildEphemeralCommandSequence(...)
  • executeEphemeralCommandSequence(...)

  • runEphemeralWithoutGoMod(...)

  • src/services/programArgs.ts

  • parseProgramArguments(rawInput)

  • src/test/runTest.ts

  • VSCode extension testing bootstrap (@vscode/test-electron).

  • src/test/suite/*.test.ts

  • Eligibility, ephemeral sequence, and argument parser coverage.

docs.title: Consolidated decisions
docs.text:
1. Primary channel: Integrated Terminal (RighToGo Terminal).
  2. Temporary cleanup default: rightogo.cleanupTemporaryDirectory = true.
  3. Cleanup guarantee: shell trap ... EXIT in ephemeral flow.
  4. LLM/MCP integration: local interface stub only in MVP.
  5. Go binary setting: rightogo.goBinaryPath with placeholder resolution and fallback.

docs.title: Known technical notes
docs.text:
• Run button visibility now relies on context key rightogo.canRunActiveGoFile.
  • New-window terminal behavior depends on VSCode command availability and has safe fallback.

docs.title: Formal V2 spec — Interactive GoSetup
docs.text: Goal: when Go is unavailable, offer safe user-profile installation instead of only showing an error.

docs.title: Business rules
docs.text:
1. Detect missing Go (goLocator failure).
  2. Fetch versions from https://go.dev/dl/?mode=json.
  3. Parse version and remove go prefix.
  4. Show options in vscode.window.showQuickPick.

  5. On approval, run installer script in Integrated Terminal.

  6. Unix/Mac:

  7. bash -c "$(curl -sSfL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubex-ecosystem/gosetup/main/go.sh')" -s install <SELECTED_VERSION>
  8. Windows:
  9. equivalent PowerShell flow using:
  10. https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubex-ecosystem/gosetup/main/go.ps1
  11. command install <SELECTED_VERSION>

docs.title: Expected V2 outcome
docs.text: • Batteries-included runtime provisioning without requiring global admin privileges.

docs.title: PILOT_PLANNING — Go QuickRun (Historical)
docs.text: This file captures the original pilot planning baseline created before implementation and naming updates.

docs.title: Pilot objective
docs.text: Build a VSCode extension to remove friction from running quick Go scripts, including standalone files that do not have go.mod.

docs.title: MVP baseline scope
docs.text:
1. Play button in editor title for eligible Go scripts.
  2. Project-mode execution when go.mod exists.
  3. Ephemeral execution when go.mod is missing:
  4. create temp dir
  5. copy file
  6. go mod init
  7. go mod tidy
  8. go run
  9. cleanup
  10. LLM/MCP bridge command (stub only).
  11. Robust error handling through VSCode messages.

docs.title: Initial architecture notes
docs.text:
• run command orchestrator.
  • goLocator with configurable binary path.
  • script eligibility service.
  • project and ephemeral runner services.
  • dedicated output/terminal strategy.
  • LLM bridge placeholder.

docs.title: Acceptance goals (pilot)
docs.text:
• Correct run behavior in project and standalone contexts.
  • Clear errors and logs.
  • Compilable and testable extension host execution.

docs.title: Follow-up evolution considered in planning
docs.text:
• Ephemeral cache by import hash.
  • Error auto-explain flow.
  • Reproducible temp snapshots.
  • GoSetup-assisted runtime onboarding.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• This planning document is intentionally preserved as a historical record.
  • Current product name and architecture details are documented in:
  • docs/dev-feed/CURRENT_STATE.md

docs.title: Scenario
docs.text: Instructors and students often need to validate Go snippets quickly without the overhead of full project scaffolding.

docs.title: How RighToGo helps
docs.text:
• Reduces setup steps.
  • Keeps focus on concepts (loops, structs, concurrency, etc.).
  • Preserves terminal interaction for input-driven exercises.

docs.title: Quick lab flow
docs.text:
1. Student creates exercise.go with package main.
  2. Runs with Play.
  3. Iterates quickly until expected output is reached.

docs.title: Example script
docs.text:
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
    var n int
    fmt.Print("Type a number: ")
    fmt.Scanln(&n)
    fmt.Printf("Double: %d\n", n*2)
}

docs.title: Outcome
docs.text: Less time spent on setup, more time on practical learning.

docs.title: Current behavior
docs.text: Execution is orchestrated by the extension and run in the Integrated Terminal.

docs.title: UX entry points
docs.text:
• Play button in editor title.
  • Command Palette.
  • Editor context menu.
  • Explorer context menu (.go file).

docs.title: Fast argument input
docs.text:
Use:

  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (With Args)

RighToGo opens a quick input box and forwards arguments to:

go run <file.go> <args...>

docs.title: Sequence with go.mod
docs.text:
cd <file-directory>
go run <file.go>

docs.title: Sequence without go.mod (ephemeral)
docs.text:
mktemp -d
cp <file.go> <temp>
cd <temp>
go mod init rightogo_temp_run
go mod tidy
go run <file.go>
# cleanup at the end (if enabled)

docs.title: Cleanup guarantee
docs.text: The ephemeral run script uses an exit-trap strategy equivalent to finally, ensuring cleanup even on failure.

docs.title: Why Integrated Terminal
docs.text:
OutputChannel is read-only and does not support stdin.

Example that needs terminal input:

package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
    var name string
    fmt.Print("Name: ")
    fmt.Scanln(&name)
    fmt.Println("Hello", name)
}

docs.title: New window execution
docs.text:
Use:

  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (New Window)

Or enable rightogo.runInNewWindowTerminalByDefault = true.

RighToGo tries to move the terminal to a new VSCode window. If unsupported in the current VSCode build, it falls back to the panel terminal.

docs.title: UI layer
docs.text:
The Play button is shown only when:

  • the context key rightogo.canRunActiveGoFile is true
  • active file satisfies Go script eligibility

docs.title: Command layer (final gate)
docs.text:
At execution time, RighToGo validates again:

  • active/target file exists
  • file is saved
  • extension is .go
  • first valid code line is package main
  • Go binary is available

docs.title: Why dual validation
docs.text:
• Better visual UX.
  • Runtime safety.
  • Lower chance of out-of-scope execution.

Defense in depth

Command-level validation prevents reliance on UI-only state.

docs.title: Script eligibility
docs.text:
RighToGo only runs files that match executable script criteria:

  • Go language file
  • .go extension
  • first valid code line is package main

docs.title: Two execution modes
Local error context
docs.text: The latest failed run snapshot is persisted for the LLM/MCP bridge flow (local MVP placeholder).

docs.title: Temporary cleanup
docs.text:
rightogo.cleanupTemporaryDirectory controls whether ephemeral directories are removed after execution.

  • default: true

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• VSCode >= 1.95.0
  • Node.js >= 16
  • pnpm (recommended for development)
  • Go installed (or configured through rightogo.goBinaryPath)

docs.title: Local development setup
docs.text:
pnpm install
pnpm run compile

Then press F5 in VSCode to open the Extension Development Host.

docs.title: Install via .vsix
docs.text:
Packaging outputs the extension artifact to bin/.

pnpm run vscode:build

Example output:

bin/rightogo-1.0.0.vsix

docs.title: Relevant build directories
docs.text:
• Compiled runtime: dist/
  • Distribution package: bin/*.vsix

Build output

This project uses dist/ as the TypeScript output directory.

docs.title: 1. Open an eligible Go script
docs.text:
The run button is shown for Go files with package main.

package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
    fmt.Println("hello from RighToGo")
}

docs.title: 2. Run from UI or context menus
docs.text:
Use the editor title button, Command Palette, or context menu.

  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script
  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (With Args)
  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (New Window)

docs.title: 3. Check output in Integrated Terminal
docs.text:
RighToGo uses RighToGo Terminal for:

  • stdout/stderr
  • stdin (interactive scripts, e.g. fmt.Scanln)

docs.title: 4. Test the analysis command (stub)
docs.text:
• RighToGo: Ask LLM About This Script

It collects:

  • active editor content
  • latest captured run error (when available)

docs.title: 5. Run automated tests
docs.text: pnpm test

docs.title: For users
docs.text:
• Keep scripts short and hypothesis-driven.
  • Prefer ephemeral mode for disposable experiments.
  • Use full projects with go.mod for persistent workflows.

docs.title: For maintainers
docs.text:
• Keep eligibility checks in both UI and command runtime.
  • Prioritize terminal-based execution for stdin compatibility.
  • Maintain coverage on decision services (eligibility, ephemeral sequence, args parser).

docs.title: Minimum observability
docs.text:
• Log relevant failures to the extension output channel.
  • Preserve concise error previews for the LLM/MCP analysis flow.

docs.title: Quality gate
docs.text:
pnpm run check-types
pnpm run lint
pnpm test

docs.title: rightogo.runScript
docs.text:
Display name:

  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script

Behavior:

  • Runs the active Go file in project or ephemeral mode.
  • Available in editor title, command palette, and context menus.

docs.title: rightogo.runScriptWithArgs
docs.text:
Display name:

  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (With Args)

Behavior:

  • Opens a quick input box for arguments.
  • Forwards args to program execution (go run <file> <args...>).

docs.title: rightogo.runScriptInNewWindow
docs.text:
Display name:

  • RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (New Window)

Behavior:

  • Executes normally, then attempts to move the terminal into a new VSCode window.
  • Falls back to panel terminal when unsupported.

docs.title: rightogo.askLlmAboutScript
docs.text:
Display name:

  • RighToGo: Ask LLM About This Script

Behavior:

  • Collects local context for future provider integration.
  • MVP uses a local no-network stub.

docs.title: Command Palette list
docs.text:
> RighToGo: Run Current Go Script
> RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (With Args)
> RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (New Window)
> RighToGo: Ask LLM About This Script

docs.title: rightogo.goBinaryPath
docs.text:
• Type: string
  • Default: ${userHome}/.go/bin/go
  • Purpose: Go binary path used by RighToGo.
  • Supported templates (resolved by extension):
  • ${userHome}
  • ${env:VARIABLE_NAME}
  • ${workspaceFolder}
  • ~ and ~/...
  • Fallback when invalid: ${userHome}/.go/bin/go, then go from PATH.

docs.title: rightogo.cleanupTemporaryDirectory
docs.text:
• Type: boolean
  • Default: true
  • Purpose: remove ephemeral execution directories after run.

docs.title: rightogo.promptForArgumentsOnRun
docs.text:
• Type: boolean
  • Default: false
  • Purpose: always prompt for CLI arguments on the default run command.

docs.title: rightogo.runInNewWindowTerminalByDefault
docs.text:
• Type: boolean
  • Default: false
  • Purpose: try moving run terminal to a new VSCode window by default.

docs.title: Example (settings.json)
docs.text:
{
  "rightogo.goBinaryPath": "/home/user/.go/bin/go",
  "rightogo.cleanupTemporaryDirectory": true,
  "rightogo.promptForArgumentsOnRun": false,
  "rightogo.runInNewWindowTerminalByDefault": false
}

docs.title: Best practices
docs.text:
• Prefer absolute Go paths in multi-runtime environments.
  • Keep cleanup enabled for day-to-day usage.
  • Disable cleanup only for targeted debugging.

docs.title: Flow 1: Quick standalone snippet
docs.text:
1. Create snippet.go.
  2. Ensure package main.
  3. Run with Play.
  4. Validate output in terminal.
  5. Discard or migrate to formal project.

docs.title: Flow 2: Dependency bug reproduction
docs.text:
1. Create minimal failing script.
  2. Run in ephemeral mode to resolve dependencies automatically.
  3. Iterate and rerun quickly.

docs.title: Flow 3: Interactive script
docs.text:
1. Build script with fmt.Scanln.
  2. Run via RighToGo.
  3. Provide input in RighToGo Terminal.

docs.title: Flow 4: Extension development cycle
docs.text:
pnpm install
pnpm run compile
pnpm test
pnpm run lint

Packaging:

pnpm run vscode:build

Expected outputs:

  • compiled JS in dist/
  • .vsix in bin/

docs.title: Flow 5: Run with arguments
docs.text:
1. Open an eligible .go file.
  2. Execute RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (With Args).
  3. Enter args (e.g. --port 8080 --name "John Doe").
  4. Validate behavior in terminal.

docs.title: Flow 6: Run in new window
docs.text:
1. Execute RighToGo: Run Current Go Script (New Window).
  2. Extension attempts to move terminal to a new window.
  3. If unsupported, execution continues in panel.
